sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The second sky blue dot also turned magenta/pink! So clicking on sky blue dots changes them to magenta. The top bar has 4 colored squares: cyan, magenta, purple, yellow. But both dots turned magenta, not cycling through the colors.
Wait - maybe the top bar shows which color to use, and the currently "selected" color is magenta. Let me check if clicking on the colored squares at the top changes the selection.
Actually, looking more carefully at the top bar: there are 4 small colored squares. Maybe they represent a color palette and I need to click on one to select a color, then click on the sky blue dots to paint them.
But both dots I clicked turned magenta without me selecting anything. Let me check if the first color in the bar (cyan) is "used up" or if there's some other indicator.
Let me try clicking on the colored squares at the top to see if that changes the paint color.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Both sky blue dots turned magenta. Let me check the top bar colors.
# Try clicking on the cyan square at top to change paint color
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 1, 'col': 1}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
